7.reason: The loop condition should be i < strlen(st) instead of i < 1000000 to avoid processing uninitialized memory beyond the string length.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  The loop condition should be i < strlen(st) instead of i < 1000000 to avoid processing uninitialized memory beyond the string length.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.2 , reason:  [Loop bound should be strlen(st) to avoid reading out-of-bounds and printing garbage. Also, need to ensure i>0 and i<len-1 when accessing st[i-1] and st[i+1].]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  Error: Loop condition should be i<strlen(st) to avoid printing beyond the string's end, causing null/garbage characters.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  The loop iterates over the entire buffer size (1,000,000) instead of the actual string length. This causes the program to read and print uninitialized memory (garbage characters) after the null terminator of the input string. It should be `i < strlen(st)` or similar.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  The loop iterates over the entire buffer size (1,000,000) instead of the actual string length. This causes the program to read and print uninitialized memory (garbage characters) after the null terminator of the input string, leading to "Buggy output too long" and incorrect output. It should be `i < strlen(st)` or similar.
